originalTitle: Sudan drone attack on key hospital killed 64 people during Eid, WHO says
title: Sad News from Sudan: A Hospital Hurt
content:
Hello from Your Africa Reporter!

Hi everyone! I'm your friend, Zara, reporting from Africa. Today, I have some sad news from a country called Sudan. It's important to talk about it so we can understand and hope for better. Sometimes grown-ups fight, and it can hurt innocent people. We need to remember that everyone deserves to be safe, especially children.

What Happened in Sudan?

In Sudan, something very sad happened at a special place, a hospital. A "drone attack" took place. That sounds like big words, right? Let's break it down. Imagine a small flying machine, like a big toy airplane, but it flies without a pilot inside. We call this a drone. Some drones are used to take pretty pictures from above, but sadly, some are used by soldiers. In this sad story, one of these drones was used to cause harm near the hospital. A "strike" means something hit the hospital or very close to it. It made a big problem. **Did you know?** Drones are sometimes used to deliver important things like medicine to places that are hard to reach! But sometimes, they are used in sad ways too.

Hospitals Are Special Places

Hospitals are very special buildings. They are places where sick people go to get better. If you fall and hurt your knee, or get a bad cough, doctors and nurses at hospitals help you. Babies are born in hospitals, and people who are very old or very sick get special care there. Hospitals are supposed to be safe places, where no one should ever be hurt. Everyone knows that hospitals are for healing people, not for fighting. They are places of peace and help. **Did you know?** The first hospitals were built thousands of years ago to help people who were sick or injured! They have always been places of hope.

Who Was Hurt?

Because of this sad event, many people were hurt, and some lost their lives. 64 people were killed. That is a very big number of people. This number includes 13 children, just like you. It's extra sad when children get hurt. Every child deserves to be safe and play. Also, two kind nurses and one helpful doctor lost their lives. Nurses and doctors work very hard to make sick people healthy again. They are like heroes in white coats. They were at the hospital doing their important job, helping people. We feel very sad for all the families who lost someone they loved.

A Special Time of Year

This sad thing happened during a special holiday called Eid. Eid is a very important and happy time for many people in Sudan and all over the world. It's a bit like Christmas or your birthday party – a time for family, friends, and celebration. People usually spend Eid together, sharing yummy food and happiness. To have something so sad happen during a happy holiday makes it even more difficult and heartbreaking for everyone. It makes the sadness even deeper. **Did you know?** There are two main Eid holidays each year, and people celebrate them with prayers, feasts, and giving gifts to each other! It's a time for joy.

Where is Sudan?

Sudan is a big country in Africa. Yes, our continent! It's very far away from many of your homes, but it's still part of our big African family. Africa has many different countries, cultures, and beautiful places. Sadly, in some parts of Sudan, grown-ups have been fighting for a while. This fighting is called a conflict. Fighting is never good, especially when it hurts innocent people and special places like hospitals. We wish for peace in Sudan. **Did you know?** Sudan is known for its ancient pyramids, which are even older than some of the pyramids in Egypt! It has a very long and interesting history.

Helping Hands from WHO

You might hear a big name called "WHO." What does that mean? WHO stands for the World Health Organization. It's a big team of people from all over the world. This team works to make sure everyone is healthy and that hospitals can help people safely. They are like giant helpers for health everywhere. WHO tells us about sad things like this because they care very much about people's health and safety. They want hospitals to be safe havens, not places where danger happens. **Did you know?** The WHO helps countries fight diseases like polio and malaria, and teaches people how to stay healthy with good food and clean water!

Why This Matters to Us

This sad news from Sudan reminds us of something very important. Hospitals must always, always be safe. They are for healing, not for harm. Children everywhere deserve to live in peace and be safe. Every child has the right to be protected and to grow up without fear. We must always wish for peace and kindness in the world. When grown-ups fight, it hurts everyone, especially the little ones who are not involved. We hope that the fighting in Sudan will stop soon, so everyone can be safe and happy again. We wish for a day when all children in Sudan can play freely.

Let's Hope for Peace

Even though this news is sad, we can still hope for good things. We can hope that the doctors and nurses will be able to help everyone who is still hurt. We can hope that the children in Sudan will soon be able to play and learn without fear. They deserve to be happy and to go to school. We can send our kind thoughts and wishes for peace to all the people in Sudan. Let's remember to be kind to each other every day, and wish for a safe world for all children. Thank you for listening, and remember to always be kind!
summary: A sad drone attack happened at a hospital in Sudan during a holiday called Eid, killing 64 people, including 13 children, 2 nurses, and 1 doctor, which the World Health Organization (WHO) reported.
whyMatters: This is important because hospitals should always be safe places for everyone, especially children, and fighting should never hurt people who are trying to help others.

originalTitle: LIVE: Real Madrid vs Atletico Madrid – La Liga
title: Big Football Fun! Real Madrid Plays Atletico Madrid!
content:
Hello, Amazing Football Fans!

Welcome, welcome, everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from the world of football! Today, we are talking about a very special game in faraway Spain. Two very famous football teams are going to play each other. It's going to be a big, big match! These two teams are called Real Madrid and Atletico Madrid. They are both from a big city in Spain called Madrid. When two teams from the same city play, it's like a big neighbourhood challenge! We call it a 'derby'. Get ready to hear all about it!

What is This Big Game About?

This big game is all about football! You might also call it soccer. Football is a super, super popular game! Lots and lots of people love to play it and watch it. It's like a big party every time there's a game! In football, two teams of players try to score goals. They use their feet to kick a round ball. They try to get the ball into the other team's net. The team that scores more goals by the end of the game wins! It's very simple but also very exciting. Kids all over Africa love to kick a ball around, just like the big players!

Meet Real Madrid!

One of the teams playing is Real Madrid. They are one of the most famous football teams in the whole wide world! Real Madrid players usually wear beautiful white shirts and shorts. Their uniform is very easy to spot on the field. This team has won many, many big trophies over the years. They are known for having amazing players and playing really well. Everyone knows Real Madrid! Did you know? Many children dream of playing for a team like Real Madrid one day!

Meet Atletico Madrid!

The other team in this big game is Atletico Madrid. They are also a super strong and famous team from Spain! Atletico Madrid players wear shirts with red and white stripes. They look very cool on the field. Atletico Madrid is known for playing with lots of energy and a big heart. They always try their very best to win every game. They are a big challenge for any team they play against. Did you know? Atletico Madrid has lots of fans who cheer very, very loudly for them!

A Special Game: The Madrid Derby!

As I told you, when two football teams come from the same city, their game is extra special. We call it a 'derby'. Imagine if your best friend from your street played on a different team in a big match – that's how exciting a derby is! Real Madrid and Atletico Madrid are both from the city of Madrid. So, their match is called the Madrid Derby. The fans from both teams get super excited and cheer very, very loudly. It's a day full of fun and friendly competition!

Where is This Game Played?

This big game is played at a very special place. It's called the Santiago Bernabéu Stadium. This is the home stadium for Real Madrid. A stadium is like a giant, super big playing field with lots and lots of seats all around it. Imagine a huge bowl where thousands of people can sit and watch the game! The Bernabéu is a very famous stadium. Did you know? Some of the biggest and most beautiful stadiums in Africa can hold many thousands of people too, just like the Bernabéu!

Why Do We Love Football So Much?

Football is wonderful! It's so much fun to watch. It makes us jump up and down with excitement when a goal is scored. It also teaches us important things, like how to work together as a team. Players run a lot and kick the ball, which means it's super good exercise! Playing football keeps you strong and healthy. Did you know? Football is the most popular sport right here in Africa! Many children, boys and girls, love to kick a ball around in their communities.

Following the Game From Far Away

Even if we are far away here in Africa, we can still follow this big game! News reporters help us by telling us everything that is happening. They write down every kick, every pass, and every goal as it happens. It's like reading a super exciting story as it's being written! We call this 'live text commentary'. It helps us imagine the game in our minds, even if we can't see it on a TV screen. It's a great way to stay connected to the football magic!

What is La Liga?

This game between Real Madrid and Atletico Madrid is part of something called 'La Liga'. 'La Liga' is like a very big competition for all the best football teams in Spain. All the top teams play against each other many times throughout the year. They collect points for winning games. The team that has the most points at the very end of the year wins the special La Liga trophy! So, every game in La Liga, especially a derby, is very important. Did you know? Many countries in Africa have their own special football leagues too!

Getting Ready for the Big Match!

Before a big game like this, people talk a lot about it. They try to guess which team might win. This is called the 'build-up' to the game. They also talk about the players and how they might play. This is called 'analysis'. It's like when you think about your favourite game before you play it! Everyone hopes their favourite team plays really well and scores lots of goals. The excitement starts even before the first kick!

Cheering for Our Teams!

It's absolutely okay and fun to have a favourite team and cheer for them! But remember, it's also very important to be kind to all the players and both teams. This is called 'sportsmanship'. Being fair and respectful makes the game even better for everyone. Whether your team wins or loses, it's about enjoying the game and celebrating the effort! Football teaches us to be good friends, even when we are competing.

Let's Have Fun With Football!

Football is a wonderful game that brings people together. Friends and families gather to watch their favourite teams. It's a game played all over the world, in big stadiums and small dusty fields, even right here in our communities in Africa! So, let's get excited for the big game between Real Madrid and Atletico Madrid! Let's enjoy the magic of football and cheer for fair play and great goals! You are all amazing football fans!
summary: Two famous Spanish football teams, Real Madrid and Atletico Madrid, are playing a super exciting 'derby' match, which is a big game between teams from the same city.
whyMatters: This game is important because it's a fun, friendly competition between two strong teams, and it shows us how exciting football can be for everyone, even for kids far away in Africa.
